message:
chore(beads): re-close 1uin + 91kt — JSONL drift again

Both beads were closed in earlier commits (1uin in c9e867d, 91kt in
083accf, both subsequently re-closed in aa58226 after a prior drift)
but have drifted back to in_progress in the JSONL. The shipped work
already exists in tree:

  - 1uin (LZF compression on RDB save) → e388065 / earlier landings.
  - 91kt (compact-type encoder selection) → eaecea1 + bfab5d3.

Edit the JSONL directly (per the documented workaround for
br-tooling drift) so `br ready` no longer keeps surfacing finished
work as outstanding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -35,7 +35,7 @@
 {"id": "frankenredis-1qdg", "title": "Implement real DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE, RELOAD, and OBJECT subcommands", "description": "# Implement real DEBUG subcommands

## What
Three DEBUG subcommands currently return OK without doing anything:
1. DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0/1: should toggle the active-expire cycle on/off
2. DEBUG RELOAD: should save + reload the dataset (equivalent to SAVE + restart load)
3. DEBUG OBJECT <key>: should return encoding, refcount, serializedlength, lru info

## Implementation

### DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE
- Add pub active_expire_enabled: bool to Store (default true)
- DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0 sets it to false, 1 sets it to true
- The runtime's active_expire_cycle checks this flag before running
- dispatch_argv needs &mut Store access (already has it)

### DEBUG RELOAD
- This is a runtime-level operation (needs to call persist_snapshot_to_disk then load_aof)
- In fr-command, we can't do this. Two options:
  a) Add a flag to Store (debug_reload_requested) that the runtime checks after dispatch
  b) Accept that DEBUG RELOAD only works from the runtime path (not from Lua)
- Recommendation: option (a) — add the flag, runtime checks it after each command batch

### DEBUG OBJECT <key>
- dispatch_argv has access to Store, which has object_encoding()
- Return format: \"Value at:0x... refcount:1 encoding:<encoding> serializedlength:<len> lru:<idle> lru_seconds_idle:<idle_s>\"
- encoding: from store.object_encoding()
- serializedlength: from store.memory_usage_for_key()
- lru/idle: from store.object_idletime()
- refcount: always 1 (same as Redis for non-shared objects)

### DEBUG JMAP
- Keep as no-op — JVM thread dump is N/A for Rust. Return OK.

## Testing
- Unit test: DEBUG SET-ACTIVE-EXPIRE 0, verify active_expire_enabled is false
- Unit test: DEBUG OBJECT <key> returns correct encoding string
- Conformance: add DEBUG OBJECT cases

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (debug_cmd)
- crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs (active_expire_enabled field)
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (check active_expire_enabled, check debug_reload_requested)", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:20:07.010370056Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T20:15:56.267730522Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T20:15:56.267460231Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-1rzz", "title": "Repair stale Beads SQLite database from JSONL", "description": "br sync --flush-only refuses to export because .beads/beads.db has fewer issues than issues.jsonl. Snapshot .beads, rebuild an isolated DB from JSONL, verify doctor/status/show, then promote without deleting old DB artifacts.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "cc_1", "created_at": "2026-04-25T01:47:41.473719041Z", "created_by": "codex-beads-repair", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T19:25:03.643357040Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-28T19:25:03.640812365Z", "close_reason": "DB recovery shipped via hs8w sister bead. Procedure documented + executed: (1) cp .beads/beads.db backup; (2) br doctor --repair (rebuilds blocked_issues_cache); (3) when 'br sync --import-only' fails with UNIQUE constraint due to duplicate-row corruption, mv the canonical .beads/beads.db{,-wal} aside; (4) br init --prefix frankenredis; (5) br sync --import-only --rebuild (imports JSONL→fresh DB cleanly). Current state verified: 'br doctor' reports sqlite.integrity_check OK, counts.db_vs_jsonl OK (806/806), sync.metadata in sync, schema.tables OK, schema.columns OK, write_probe OK. Snapshots preserved at .beads/beads.db.backup_orphan_recovery_1777347889 and .beads/beads.db.corrupt_orphan_recovery_1777347912 (gitignored). The 'snapshot, rebuild isolated, verify, promote' acceptance criterion has been met end-to-end.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
-{"id": "frankenredis-1uin", "title": "[HIGH] r73v sub-task (c): LZF compression on RDB save", "description": "Follow-up to br-frankenredis-r73v: implement upstream-compatible LZF compression on the fr-persist RDB write side. Today fr-persist::rdb_encode_string emits raw length-prefixed strings; upstream prefers LZF (encoding byte 0xC3 with the 0x40 special-encoding flag) for strings >= 16 bytes (configurable via rdbcompression). fr-persist already DEcompresses LZF on read (see lzf_decompress in fr-persist/src/lib.rs:1212). Implement the matching LZF compressor (~80 lines, mirrors upstream's lzf_c.c::lzf_compress). Wire into rdb_encode_string with a heuristic threshold + 'compressed-fits' check. Acceptance: encode/decode round-trip is identity AND fr-persist's emitted dump.rdb loads cleanly under upstream redis-server when long strings are present.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "cc_1", "created_at": "2026-04-28T19:43:46.321875790Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-29T02:04:30.200053Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-29T02:04:30.200053Z", "close_reason": "Shipped in 9dee1ef: pure-Rust LZF compressor + 4 proptest properties + live oracle gate. Re-closure after JSONL drift in 1458723 reverted prior close (083accf cycle)."}
+{"id": "frankenredis-1uin", "title": "[HIGH] r73v sub-task (c): LZF compression on RDB save", "description": "Follow-up to br-frankenredis-r73v: implement upstream-compatible LZF compression on the fr-persist RDB write side. Today fr-persist::rdb_encode_string emits raw length-prefixed strings; upstream prefers LZF (encoding byte 0xC3 with the 0x40 special-encoding flag) for strings >= 16 bytes (configurable via rdbcompression). fr-persist already DEcompresses LZF on read (see lzf_decompress in fr-persist/src/lib.rs:1212). Implement the matching LZF compressor (~80 lines, mirrors upstream's lzf_c.c::lzf_compress). Wire into rdb_encode_string with a heuristic threshold + 'compressed-fits' check. Acceptance: encode/decode round-trip is identity AND fr-persist's emitted dump.rdb loads cleanly under upstream redis-server when long strings are present.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "cc_1", "created_at": "2026-04-28T19:43:46.321875790Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-29T03:30:09.548708Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-29T03:30:09.548708Z", "design": {}}
 {"id": "frankenredis-1v8b", "title": "Implement real REPLICAOF TCP client that connects to primary and completes PSYNC handshake", "description": "# REPLICAOF TCP Client

## What
When `REPLICAOF host port` is executed (or --replicaof CLI flag), FrankenRedis should actually connect to the specified primary via TCP, complete the PSYNC handshake, and enter replication mode.

## Current State
The existing `drive_replica_sync()` in fr-server/src/main.rs has the connection scaffolding including PING, AUTH, REPLCONF, and PSYNC handshake steps. The `ReplicaPrimaryConnection` struct exists. The `replication_follow_up_bytes()` function exists. The issue is that REPLICAOF command in fr-runtime returns OK but does not trigger the actual connection — it only sets config state.

## Implementation
1. In fr-runtime, REPLICAOF should set `server.replicaof_host` and `server.replicaof_port` fields (add if missing)
2. In fr-server main loop, check if replicaof is configured but no ReplicaPrimaryConnection exists → initiate connection
3. The existing `drive_replica_sync()` already handles the handshake FSM — verify it works with a real Redis primary
4. After FULLRESYNC: receive RDB snapshot bytes, decode with fr_persist::decode_rdb, apply via apply_rdb_entries_to_store
5. After CONTINUE: receive backlog bytes, parse as RESP commands, execute through runtime
6. Handle connection failure: set retry timer, log error, try again after backoff

## Unit Tests (in fr-runtime)
- Test 1: REPLICAOF host port sets config fields correctly
- Test 2: REPLICAOF NO ONE clears config and stops replication
- Test 3: INFO replication shows role:slave when replicaof is set
- Test 4: INFO replication shows master_link_status:down before connection succeeds

## Integration Tests (in fr-server tests)
- Test 5: Start legacy redis-server, start frankenredis with --replicaof, verify PING/REPLCONF/PSYNC handshake completes (check INFO for master_link_status:up)
- Test 6: After handshake, SET on primary → GET on replica returns the value
- Test 7: Connection drops → replica detects and schedules retry

## Logging
- Log each handshake step with timestamp: "REPL: sending PING to primary", "REPL: received PONG", etc.
- Log FULLRESYNC/CONTINUE decision and offset
- Log snapshot size and apply time
- Log connection failures with retry schedule

## Files
- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs (REPLICAOF command handler, config fields)
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs (connection initiation, snapshot apply)
- crates/fr-server/tests/replication_e2e.rs (integration tests)", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.889343993Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T05:22:51.278623941Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T05:22:51.278359060Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-1zxx", "title": "Add benchmark regression gate to CI/scripts pipeline", "description": "# Benchmark Regression Gate

## What
A script that runs the benchmark suite, compares against checked-in baselines, and fails if p99 degrades by more than 10% or throughput drops by more than 15%.

## Why
Performance regressions are silent killers. The Performance Doctrine in AGENTS.md requires "gate persistence overhead and replication-lag regressions" but no gate exists.

## Implementation
1. scripts/benchmark_gate.sh:
   - Start frankenredis in background
   - Run fr_benchmark with standard workloads
   - Compare against baselines/*.json
   - Report deltas
   - Exit non-zero if regression detected
2. Configurable thresholds via env vars
3. Output: delta report artifact in artifacts/benchmark/

## Depends On
- Benchmark harness (fr_benchmark binary)
- Initial baselines recorded", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:10:23.224067917Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T22:56:05.922840524Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T22:56:05.922656586Z", "close_reason": "Implemented benchmark regression gate script, docs, and guarded workflow integration", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-1zxx", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-gess", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.702954088Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
@@ -199,7 +199,7 @@
 {"id": "frankenredis-8xmq", "title": "Downgrade FEATURE_PARITY.md replication status to honest assessment", "description": "# Honest Replication Status

## What
Change FEATURE_PARITY.md "Replication baseline | parity_green" to "parity_gap" or "in_progress" until cross-process replication actually works.

## Why
The current "parity_green" rating for replication is dishonest. The state machine logic works in isolation but no bytes flow between processes. Honesty about project status is a prerequisite for good engineering.

## Implementation
1. Update FEATURE_PARITY.md: change replication row status to "in_progress"
2. Update notes to honestly describe what works (FSM logic, PSYNC decision math, offset tracking) and what does not (actual TCP replication)
3. After replication epic is complete, upgrade back to parity_green with evidence

## No Dependencies
This should be done immediately, before any replication implementation work.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:17.272701785Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T18:52:37.119459043Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T18:52:37.119284593Z", "close_reason": "Updated FEATURE_PARITY.md replication row from parity_green to in_progress with honest assessment of what works (TCP replication, PSYNC, FULLRESYNC) and what does not (runtime REPLICAOF command, replica-of-replica chains, FR-to-FR E2E, diskless replication)", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-90iz", "title": "Align XREAD BLOCK timeout validation with Redis integer-ms semantics", "description": "Redis requires XREAD/XREADGROUP BLOCK to be an integer millisecond timeout and rejects out-of-range values. FrankenRedis currently validates BLOCK as a float in fr-command and uses saturating_add in fr-server, which can accept fractional or oversized inputs incorrectly.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-06T23:04:58.923207545Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-06T23:17:17.667520443Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-06T23:17:17.667268236Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
-{"id": "frankenredis-91kt", "title": "[HIGH] r73v sub-task (a-ext): byte-identical re-emit (compact-type encoder selection)", "description": "Stretch acceptance for br-frankenredis-r73v: 're-emit byte-identical on save'. The 9c460ed live_redis_rdb_reemit_loadrdb_matches_upstream gate proves STATE-equivalence cross-impl, but the bytes differ because fr-persist::encode_rdb always emits canonical type tags (RDB_TYPE_LIST=1, RDB_TYPE_SET=2, RDB_TYPE_HASH=4, RDB_TYPE_ZSET_2=5) where upstream picks the COMPACT form (LIST_QUICKLIST_2=18, SET_INTSET=11, SET_LISTPACK=20, HASH_LISTPACK=16, ZSET_LISTPACK=17) for shapes that fit its size thresholds. Wire fr-persist::encode_rdb to mirror upstream's compact-type selection (re-using the encoder helpers that already exist in fr-store::dump_key for DUMP/RESTORE). Acceptance: encode_rdb(decode_rdb(upstream_dump)) == upstream_dump for all shapes within compact thresholds.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-28T19:43:46.515668824Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-29T01:35:05.484498225Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-29T01:35:05.482695502Z", "close_reason": "Shipped in eaecea1: encode_rdb_with_options accepts CompactRdbThresholds (defaults mirror Redis 7.2 vanilla: hash_max_lp_entries=512/value=64, set_max_intset_entries=512, set_max_lp_entries=128/value=64, zset_max_lp_entries=128/value=64, list_max_lp_size=8192). Compact-shape selection helpers: encode_compact_set_intset (rejects non-canonical integers like '+1' that wouldn't round-trip byte-identically), encode_compact_set_listpack, encode_compact_hash_listpack, encode_compact_zset_listpack (rejects NaN), encode_compact_list_quicklist2 (single-PACKED-node + per-PLAIN-node fallback). encode_rdb default-options output stays byte-identical for backward compat. 6 new tests including the 91kt acceptance gate compact_encoder_round_trips_upstream_shaped_seeds which loads fuzz_rdb_decoder corpus seeds, decodes, re-encodes with compact options, and pins the same compact type tag is selected. cargo test -p fr-persist 150/150; clippy --workspace --all-targets clean.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}

The 9c460ed live_redis_rdb_reemit_loadrdb_matches_upstream gate proves STATE-equivalence cross-impl, but the bytes differ because fr-persist::encode_rdb always emits canonical type tags (RDB_TYPE_LIST=1, RDB_TYPE_SET=2, RDB_TYPE_HASH=4, RDB_TYPE_ZSET_2=5) where upstream picks the COMPACT form (LIST_QUICKLIST_2=18, SET_INTSET=11, SET_LISTPACK=20, HASH_LISTPACK=16, ZSET_LISTPACK=17) for shapes that fit its size thresholds. Wire fr-persist::encode_rdb to mirror upstream's compact-type selection (re-using the encoder helpers that already exist in fr-store::dump_key for DUMP/RESTORE). Acceptance: encode_rdb(decode_rdb(upstream_dump)) == upstream_dump for all shapes within compact thresholds.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-28T19:43:46.515668824Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-29T03:30:09.548708Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "closed_at": "2026-04-29T03:30:09.548708Z", "design": {}}

-{"id": "frankenredis-ian4", "title": "[HIGH] s5su follow-up: 20-fixture upstream stream RDB corpus + --loadrdb round-trip gate + structured fuzzer", "description": "Parent br-frankenredis-s5su shipped at command + DUMP/RESTORE level (RDB type 15/19/21 decode + type 21 encode; live_redis_core_stream_matches_runtime 167/167; live_redis_stream_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream green). The file-level acceptance criteria (20 upstream-produced .rdb fixtures × decode + re-encode + 'redis-server --loadrdb' XINFO STREAM FULL identity; structure-aware fuzzer for fuzz_rdb_structured.rs) remain open. Steps: (1) Build the fixture corpus by spawning vendored redis-server, populating each shape (entries, deleted entries, multiple groups, multiple consumers, non-zero PEL, non-zero seen/active_time), running BGSAVE, capturing dump.rdb. (2) Add fr-conformance harness that loops the corpus through fr-persist::decode_rdb + re-emit + cross-load. (3) Extend fuzz_rdb_structured.rs with arbitrary::Arbitrary stream generator, gate end-to-end upstream loadback behind REQUIRE_UPSTREAM_RDB_LOAD=1.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "cod_1", "created_at": "2026-04-28T03:41:04.230661384Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T19:58:55.636923825Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-28T19:58:55.635081842Z", "close_reason": "Completed: stream RDB corpus loadback gate and structured fuzzer upstream-load option landed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-ian4","title":"[HIGH] s5su follow-up: 20-fixture upstream stream RDB corpus + --loadrdb round-trip gate + structured fuzzer","description":"Parent br-frankenredis-s5su shipped at command + DUMP/RESTORE level (RDB type 15/19/21 decode + type 21 encode; live_redis_core_stream_matches_runtime 167/167; live_redis_stream_dump_restore_roundtrip_matches_upstream green). The file-level acceptance criteria (20 upstream-produced .rdb fixtures × decode + re-encode + 'redis-server --loadrdb' XINFO STREAM FULL identity; structure-aware fuzzer for fuzz_rdb_structured.rs) remain open. Steps: (1) Build the fixture corpus by spawning vendored redis-server, populating each shape (entries, deleted entries, multiple groups, multiple consumers, non-zero PEL, non-zero seen/active_time), running BGSAVE, capturing dump.rdb. (2) Add fr-conformance harness that loops the corpus through fr-persist::decode_rdb + re-emit + cross-load. (3) Extend fuzz_rdb_structured.rs with arbitrary::Arbitrary stream generator, gate end-to-end upstream loadback behind REQUIRE_UPSTREAM_RDB_LOAD=1.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-28T03:41:04.230661384Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:58:55.636923825Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T19:58:55.635081842Z","close_reason":"Completed: stream RDB corpus loadback gate and structured fuzzer upstream-load option landed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-icsm", "title": "EPIC: Establish real performance baseline with redis-benchmark-compatible harness and p50/p95/p99 latency tracking", "description": "# Performance Evidence Epic

## Why This Is Critical
The README describes "Deterministic Latency Replication Core (DLRC)" as the "Crown-Jewel Innovation" and claims "profile-proven performance" with "p95/p99 latency tracking." Yet the repo contains ZERO throughput benchmarks, ZERO latency percentile measurements, and an empty baselines/ directory. The only checked-in artifacts measure conformance test execution speed (~1.3ms), not Redis workload performance.

This is the single most dangerous gap because performance is the stated reason this project exists as a differentiator from legacy Redis. Without evidence, DLRC is marketing copy, not engineering.

## What Success Looks Like
- A redis-benchmark-compatible harness that measures ops/sec, latency histograms (p50/p95/p99/p999), and memory usage under mixed workloads
- Baseline numbers checked into baselines/ with comparison against legacy Redis on identical workloads
- CI-gatable regression detection: any commit that degrades p99 by >10% fails the gate
- The README performance claims are either proven or honestly revised

## Architecture
The benchmark harness should be a new binary in fr-server or a dedicated crate that:
1. Connects via real TCP to a running frankenredis instance
2. Runs configurable workloads (SET/GET mixed, pipeline depth, key sizes)
3. Records latency histograms using HdrHistogram or equivalent
4. Outputs JSON artifacts compatible with the existing artifacts/ structure
5. Compares against baselines/ and reports deltas

## Dependencies
All sub-tasks feed into this epic. The benchmark harness must exist before any optimization work can be proven.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:10:22.979891472Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-10T02:20:30.057699153Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-10T02:20:30.057479858Z", "close_reason": "All sub-tasks completed: benchmark harness (bxlo), baselines (gess), regression gate (1zxx) all closed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-icsm", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-1zxx", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.512824074Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-icsm", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-bxlo", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.348066219Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-icsm", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-gess", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.429198141Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

 {"id": "frankenredis-r71v", "title": "[HIGH] Reality-check vs upstream Redis 7+: CLUSTER FORGET/COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS, ACL GETUSER selectors, XADD MINID/LIMIT, XSETID ENTRIESADDED", "description": "Run a focused parity audit against vendored redis-server (7.2.4) for the Redis 7-era surface that hasn't been live-oracle exercised yet. Specifically:

(a) CLUSTER subcommands beyond MYID/SHARDS/SLOTS/INFO: FORGET, COUNT-FAILURE-REPORTS, RESET, BUMPEPOCH, FLUSHSLOTS, ADDSLOTSRANGE/DELSLOTSRANGE, LINKS. Verify error wording when cluster mode is disabled and arity errors against upstream's exact phrasing.
(b) ACL GETUSER selectors field (Redis 7.0+ multi-selector support): the field is structurally present in our reply but the channel/key/command vectors per selector may not match upstream when the user has zero selectors vs N>0 selectors.
(c) XADD MINID and LIMIT options (Redis 7.0+): we accept them but their effect on stream trimming + the +OK/(nil)/error reply shape needs upstream verification.
(d) XSETID with ENTRIESADDED and MAXDELETEDID arguments (Redis 7.2+).

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: spawn vendored redis-server, send each combination via the existing live-oracle harness, surface every divergence as a follow-up [HIGH] bead.  {"id": "frankenredis-r72v", "title": "[HIGH] RESP3 protocol parity: HELLO 3 push-frame routing + map/set/double/big-number types in real handlers", "description": "Our parser learned RESP3 reply types via ParserConfig::allow_resp3 (br-frankenredis-ozcx), but the runtime handlers haven't been audited end-to-end for the RESP3-only reply shapes:

(a) HELLO 3: server response should include a Map instead of an Array for the metadata; verify client receives '%' framing not '*'.
(b) Push frames ('>' prefix): pub/sub messages, client-side caching invalidations, and tracking redirects should switch to push frames when the session's resp_protocol_version == 3. Today we still emit Arrays even after HELLO 3.
(c) Map ('%') replies for HGETALL/CONFIG GET/XINFO STREAM in RESP3 — upstream switches to Map; verify we mirror that.
(d) Set ('~') replies for SMEMBERS/SINTER/SUNION/SDIFF in RESP3.
(e) Double (',') replies for ZSCORE/ZADD INCR in RESP3.
(f) Big number ('(') support for very large counters.
(g) Verbatim string ('=') for INFO/CLUSTER NODES.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend live-oracle harness to negotiate HELLO 3 and re-run the relevant cases, comparing against upstream's RESP3 wire frames.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "cod-frankenredis", "created_at": "2026-04-25T00:05:00Z", "created_by": "cc-redis", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T03:50:23.174897054Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-25T03:50:23.173045710Z", "close_reason": "Completed in fc3d541", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["hello", "protocol", "push", "resp3"]}

 {"id": "frankenredis-r74v", "title": "[HIGH] Pub/Sub scalability + keyspace notifications: SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH (sharded), CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS, K/E notify masks", "description": "(a) Sharded pub/sub (Redis 7+): SSUBSCRIBE/SUNSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH against shard-channels. CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS / SHARDNUMSUB introspection. Our implementation routes shard pub/sub through the same global hash-table as PUBLISH — needs a separate shard map keyed by hash-slot owner.
(b) Keyspace notifications: CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events 'KEA' should turn on __keyspace@<db>__:<key> and __keyevent@<db>__:<event> message generation for every applicable command. Today our notify mask is parsed but only a subset of commands actually fire events. Wire the full Redis event taxonomy (string/list/set/zset/hash/stream/expire/evicted/new/loaded/trim).
(c) NUMSUB regression: NUMSUB on a single channel with N subscribers should return [channel, N] not [channel, 0] when subscriptions are split across multiple processes (matters once we add cluster mode).
(d) PUBSUB NUMPAT counts unique pattern subscriptions across all clients.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 6: drive scenarios from a multi-client harness (extends fr_p2c_006 replication journey infrastructure).", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-25T00:05:00Z", "created_by": "cc-redis", "updated_at": "2026-04-26T05:30:00Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-26T05:30:00Z", "close_reason": "Sharded pub/sub (SSUBSCRIBE/SPUBLISH/SUNSUBSCRIBE/CLUSTER PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS/SHARDNUMSUB) already wired.  {"id": "frankenredis-r75v", "title": "[HIGH] Lua scripting parity audit: EVAL/EVALSHA/EVAL_RO + FUNCTION CREATE/LOAD/CALL/FLUSH/DUMP/RESTORE + script-side redis.* surface", "description": "Reality-check our Lua execution path against vendored redis-server:

(a) EVAL with multiple keys + args, error propagation, return value coercion (status reply, integer, bulk string, nested array, redis-error vs runtime-error).
(b) EVALSHA cache hit/miss, SCRIPT EXISTS, SCRIPT LOAD, SCRIPT FLUSH ASYNC/SYNC.
(c) EVAL_RO + EVALSHA_RO read-only enforcement: any write call inside RO must return -READONLY.
(d) FUNCTION LOAD with the !lua engine + meta header, FUNCTION CALL, FUNCTION FLUSH, FUNCTION DUMP/RESTORE binary format (Redis 7+).
(e) The redis.* surface inside scripts: redis.call, redis.pcall, redis.error_reply, redis.status_reply, redis.sha1hex, redis.log, redis.replicate_commands(), redis.breakpoint(), redis.debug(), redis.set_repl(), KEYS / ARGV indexing semantics.
(f) MULTI inside EVAL: should be rejected; EXEC inside EVAL should error.
(g) FCALL_RO + script-flag #!lua flags=no-writes / allow-stale / allow-oom / allow-cross-slot-keys.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend core_scripting.json + core_function.json with each combination, run live-oracle diff.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-25T00:05:00Z", "created_by": "cc-redis", "updated_at": "2026-04-26T05:30:00Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-26T05:30:00Z", "close_reason": "Major slices shipped: (c) EVAL_RO/EVALSHA_RO read-only enforcement covered by existing tests. (g) Redis 7+ #!lua flags=no-writes shebang now honoured by eval_cmd/evalsha_cmd. (e) redis.* surface (call/pcall/error_reply/status_reply/sha1hex/log/replicate_commands/breakpoint/debug/set_repl/KEYS/ARGV) all wired. (a) EVAL multi-key/arg + return-coercion covered. (b) EVALSHA cache + SCRIPT EXISTS/LOAD/FLUSH ASYNC|SYNC covered. (f) MULTI inside EVAL rejected. (d) FUNCTION LOAD/CALL/FLUSH/DUMP/RESTORE binary format tracked under r83v.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["eval", "function", "lua", "scripting"]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-r80v", "title": "[HIGH] RESP3 push frame routing: pub/sub messages, client-side caching invalidations, tracking redirects must use '>' framing on RESP3 sessions", "description": "After HELLO 3, upstream Redis switches asynchronous-server-message paths from RESP2 Array to RESP3 Push frame ('>' prefix per https://github.com/redis/redis-specifications/blob/master/protocol/RESP3.md#push-type). Today our runtime continues to emit Arrays:

(a) PUBSUB messages: when a SUBSCRIBE'd client receives a PUBLISH, the ["message", channel, payload] reply must be a Push, not an Array. Same for ["smessage", ...] (sharded), ["pmessage", pattern, channel, payload], ["subscribe"|"unsubscribe"|"psubscribe"|"punsubscribe"|"ssubscribe"|"sunsubscribe", channel, count].

(b) Client-side caching invalidations: with CLIENT TRACKING ON in RESP3 mode, the server pushes ["invalidate", [keys...]] frames to the tracking redirect target on key mutation. Today our runtime queues these on the wrong stream and as Array.

(c) Server-side notifications (when more land): keyspace notifications via CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events route through the same pub/sub layer; verify the push framing follows.

Apply /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 1: extend the live-oracle harness to negotiate HELLO 3 on the dedicated connection used for these cases, then assert the wire frame on the receiving side starts with the '>' byte under RESP3 (and '*' under RESP2).

Scope guard: this bead only covers the framing of asynchronous-server-message paths. Synchronous request/reply RESP3 promotions (HGETALL→Map, SMEMBERS→Set, ZSCORE→Double) are tracked separately under br-frankenredis-r72v.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "cod-frankenredis", "created_at": "2026-04-25T03:30:00Z", "created_by": "cc-redis", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T05:15:56.813244354Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-25T05:15:56.811371439Z", "close_reason": "Completed in 5c83cec", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["client-tracking", "pubsub", "push", "resp3"]}

 {"id": "frankenredis-xmev", "title": "[HIGH] CONFIG parity follow-ups: ephemeral comparator, multi-pattern order, case-insensitive echo, error template, notify-keyspace canonicalization", "description": "Follow-up to br-frankenredis-2di1 sub-tasks not landed by 393cfcf. (a) CONFIG GET port/dir/maxmemory ephemeral values: live_oracle_config_get_matches canonicalizer needs ignore-list or harness-derived expected values. (b) CONFIG GET multi-pattern reply order canonicalization (sort like HGETALL). (c) CONFIG GET case-insensitive reply key name — upstream returns the requested casing verbatim. (d) CONFIG SET error-wording template alignment with upstream's 'ERR CONFIG SET failed (possibly related to argument <name>) - <reason>' format for unknown-parameter / non-integer / negative-integer / policy-invalid cases. (h) notify-keyspace-events canonicalization: ours echoes as-written, upstream normalizes to AKE order. Live-oracle currently XFAILs config_get_star_returns_array and config_get_notify_keyspace_events_after_set (live_redis_core_config_matches_runtime in crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs).", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-28T02:24:30.064144127Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T02:37:15.970948798Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-28T02:37:15.968178441Z", "close_reason": "Sub-task (h) notify-keyspace-events canonicalization landed in 3044ee4: CONFIG SET stores upstream-AKE-order canonical form, keyspace_events_to_string moves 'n' inside the per-class else branch to match upstream notify.c. Sub-tasks (a) port/dir/maxmemory ephemeral, (b) multi-pattern reply order, (c) case-insensitive reply key were already handled by canonical_config_get_entries (VOLATILE_KEYS + sort + lowercase). Sub-task (d) error-wording template for unknown-parameter / numeric-range / policy-invalid CONFIG SET cases is broad cleanup with no specific XFAIL coverage; tracked separately if/when a live oracle case surfaces it.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
-{"id": "frankenredis-xmr8", "title": "fr-persist: type-21 consumer-group payload entries_read approximation under-counts", "description": "Surfaced by /mock-code-finder pass. encode_consumer_group at crates/fr-persist/src/rdb_stream.rs was writing group.pending.len() for the entries_read slot in upstream STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 payloads synthesized from in-memory state. Upstream's entries_read is the cumulative count of entries the group has read (including acked ones), used by streamReplyWithRange + XINFO STREAM FULL for the lag metric. pending.len() is the unacked-only count which drops back to zero on every XACK. Using the latter inflates the upstream-side lag every time a consumer acks anything.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-28T20:53:35.462633491Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-28T20:53:43.497656815Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-28T20:53:43.495727191Z", "close_reason": "Shipped in 433c413: encode_consumer_group at crates/fr-persist/src/rdb_stream.rs:147 now emits the upstream SCG_INVALID_ENTRIES_READ sentinel (-1 cast to u64, encoded as 0x81 + 8 x 0xFF) for the entries_read slot when fr-persist synthesizes the type-21 payload from in-memory state. Upstream's loadrdb path then falls back to streamEstimateDistanceFromFirstEverEntry for lag computation rather than trusting the under-counted pending.len() approximation. Locked in by new unit test encode_consumer_group_writes_scg_invalid_entries_read_sentinel which byte-scans the encoded payload for the 9-byte sentinel sequence and explicitly asserts the entries_read slot byte is 0x81 (NOT 0x02 = old buggy pending.len()=2). cargo test -p fr-persist 138/138 green.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-xmr8","title":"fr-persist: type-21 consumer-group payload entries_read approximation under-counts","description":"Surfaced by /mock-code-finder pass. encode_consumer_group at crates/fr-persist/src/rdb_stream.rs was writing group.pending.len() for the entries_read slot in upstream STREAM_LISTPACKS_3 payloads synthesized from in-memory state. Upstream's entries_read is the cumulative count of entries the group has read (including acked ones), used by streamReplyWithRange + XINFO STREAM FULL for the lag metric. pending.len() is the unacked-only count which drops back to zero on every XACK. Using the latter inflates the upstream-side lag every time a consumer acks anything.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:53:35.462633491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:53:43.497656815Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T20:53:43.495727191Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 433c413: encode_consumer_group at crates/fr-persist/src/rdb_stream.rs:147 now emits the upstream SCG_INVALID_ENTRIES_READ sentinel (-1 cast to u64, encoded as 0x81 + 8 x 0xFF) for the entries_read slot when fr-persist synthesizes the type-21 payload from in-memory state. Upstream's loadrdb path then falls back to streamEstimateDistanceFromFirstEverEntry for lag computation rather than trusting the under-counted pending.len() approximation. Locked in by new unit test encode_consumer_group_writes_scg_invalid_entries_read_sentinel which byte-scans the encoded payload for the 9-byte sentinel sequence and explicitly asserts the entries_read slot byte is 0x81 (NOT 0x02 = old buggy pending.len()=2). cargo test -p fr-persist 138/138 green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-xvvu", "title": "Wire real counters into INFO stats section", "description": "# Wire real counters into INFO stats section

## What
Replace hardcoded zeros in fr-command's info() stats section with real Store counter values. After the foundation counter beads land, these fields become simple reads.

## Fields to fix (fr-command/src/lib.rs, info() function, stats section ~line 8290):
1. expired_keys → store.stat_expired_keys
2. expired_stale_perc → store.stat_expired_stale_perc (or compute from cycle stats)
3. expired_time_cap_reached_count → 0 is acceptable (cap not implemented)
4. expire_cycle_cpu_milliseconds → store.stat_expire_cycle_cpu_milliseconds
5. evicted_keys → store.stat_evicted_keys
6. total_keys_expired → store.stat_expired_keys (same counter)
7. total_keys_evicted → store.stat_evicted_keys (same counter)
8. keyspace_hits → store.stat_keyspace_hits
9. keyspace_misses → store.stat_keyspace_misses

## Fields in clients section (~line 8198):
10. blocked_clients → store.stat_blocked_clients (populated by server)
11. tracking_clients → 0 (acceptable until CLIENT TRACKING is fully wired)

## Fields in memory section (~line 8218):
12. maxmemory → store.maxmemory_bytes_live (populated by runtime)

## Fields in CPU section (~line 8372):
These require platform-specific code (reading /proc/self/stat on Linux). Lower priority — acceptable to leave as 0 with a comment explaining why.

## Depends on
- frankenredis-xf0u (keyspace hit/miss counters)
- frankenredis-jg50 (expired/evicted counters)
- frankenredis-yf1j (runtime context fields)

## Testing
- Conformance: add INFO stats cases that verify non-zero counters after operations
- Unit test: set + get key, verify keyspace_hits incremented in INFO output

## Files to modify
- crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs (info() function stats/clients/memory sections)", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:19:16.554565415Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-03T03:07:43.430353494Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-03T03:07:43.430099193Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-xvvu", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-jg50", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:21:34.361555156Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-xvvu", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-xf0u", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:21:34.243078331Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-xvvu", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-yf1j", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-03T00:21:34.479597319Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-xy1q", "title": "[HIGH] XADD auto-ID + broad stream divergences — fixture clock vs upstream wall clock", "description": "Surfaced by live_redis_core_stream_matches_runtime: 48/218 divergences; the majority stem from XADD auto-generated IDs using the fixture now_ms (e.g. '1000-0') while upstream redis-server stamps entries with its wall-clock ms (e.g. '1777004357777-0'). Dependent commands (XRANGE, XREAD, XLEN after deletion, XPENDING) then cascade mismatches because they reference those IDs. Fix options: (a) add stream-specific canonicalization that normalizes '*' auto-IDs in the expected/actual frames before compare; (b) regenerate the fixture after a harness upgrade that stubs now_ms on the upstream side via EVAL or CLIENT NO-EVICT; (c) split the stream fixture into 'deterministic-id' (explicit ms-seq) vs 'auto-id' suites and only run the former under live oracle. Also: xadd_explicit_higher_id — ours accepts a new entry where upstream rejects with 'ERR The ID specified in XADD is equal or smaller than the target stream top item', independent of the clock issue.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-24T04:21:15.953055702Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T22:38:36.531218175Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T22:38:36.529285866Z", "close_reason": "Already resolved: live_redis_core_stream_matches_runtime passes 100% under FR_CONFORMANCE_LIVE_ORACLE_STRICT=1 (fixture-clock canonicalizer on upstream stream IDs landed previously).", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
